name: Active Lifestyle
description: Aspirational-active UGC: a real-feeling person doing a real activity (cycling, padel, gym, yoga, running, hiking, surfing, skiing) with subtle product placement. The product is visible 60-80% of shots but never hero-framed until the optional outro beat. Converts via identification — 'this is the kind of person I want to be → I will buy what they use.' 12-25s, 9:16. Reference-required when the product is a real brand (athletic apparel logo, cycling-shoe brand, smart-watch face, supplement label) — same hard gate as before-after-product. AI must never improvise a brand mark on athletic gear.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter for the persona + activity establishing keyframes. Multi-ref WITH the user-supplied product photo / brand asset for any frame where a branded item is visible (shoes, watch face, bottle label, jersey logo). Real-environment lighting cue is mandatory — no studio key-light.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s clips (3-6 total), generate_audio: false. Kling drives the activity-motion specifics: pedal cadence, padel swing follow-through, lifting cadence, running stride form, yoga flow transition. Slow-mo bias on the 18-25s satisfaction beat (smile / breath / horizon / refill).
stackSummary.voiceover: Usually OFF. Diegetic sport sounds (breath, footstrike, chain, ball-pop, weight-clank) plus the music bed carry the format. If VO is used at all → ONE conversational line at the outro, ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2, language follows the brief.
stackSummary.captions: Usually OFF. If used: MinimalCaptions for an outro single line only — never full-video Hormozi captions, that pushes the piece into 'ad' pattern-recognition.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single energetic-but-not-hyped bed, full-length. Three audio profiles: instrumental electronic 110-130 BPM (driven sports — cycling, running, gym), acoustic indie / driving-acoustic 90-110 BPM (serene flow — yoga, hiking, surf), hip-hop 100-110 BPM (gym, padel). Avoid 'ad-pumped' EDM stings — they tip the format from lifestyle to commercial.
stackSummary.overlay: Brand title-card on the last 1.5s only (Remotion overlay, lower-third or centered). No mid-video brand overlays — they break the diegetic feel.

constants.structure: 0-3s establishing pull-back-to-action → 3-18s activity with product naturally in frame → 18-25s satisfaction beat
constants.productVisibilityRange: 60-80% of shots; never hero-framed until optional outro

variationAxes: sport (cycling / padel / gym / yoga / running / hiking / surfing / skiing)
environment (urban / coastal / mountain / studio-gym / park / trail)
doNotCopyLiterally: Sport choice, exact product, environment, persona, prompts. Vibe reference only — the discipline is product-not-hero, not the specific activity.
referenceNotes: Product / brand reference (logo, packaging, watch face, shoe photo, jersey print) is REQUIRED whenever a real brand is in the brief. The reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) refuses if no reference exists at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/. AI-improvised branding on athletic apparel, watches, or supplements never reads right — the logo geometry is wrong, the typeface is wrong, and viewers spot it instantly. User can override with explicit 'generate without reference, I understand the quality will be worse'; logged as stage: 'no-ref-consent'.
